title: Creating GCP SQL instances without requiring TLS is security-sensitive
description: GCP SQL instances that do not require TLS allow unencrypted connections, exposing sensitive data to interception and tampering. Enforcing TLS ensures data in transit is protected and meets compliance requirements.
message: SQL instance does not require TLS. Enforce encrypted connections for all database access.
impact: Unencrypted database connections can be intercepted, leading to data breaches and regulatory violations.
recommendation: Configure all GCP SQL instances to require SSL/TLS for client connections. Disallow unencrypted access.

noncompliant.description: Instance allows unencrypted connections, exposing data in transit.

compliant.description: Instance requires SSL/TLS for all connections, protecting data in transit.

devsecops.security: TLS protects database credentials and sensitive data from interception.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD should enforce 'require_ssl = true' for all SQL instances.
devsecops.compliance: Encrypted connections are required by PCI DSS, HIPAA, and other standards.

why_is_this_an_issue: Allowing unencrypted database connections exposes sensitive data to attackers and violates compliance requirements.
how_to_fix: Set 'require_ssl = true' in all SQL instance configurations. Test connections to ensure encryption is enforced.

security_mappings.CWE: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
security_mappings.CERT: MSC12-C: Use SSL/TLS for sensitive communication
